label: 2026-06-03 close
title: Daily FI Market Tape | 2026-06-03 close
summary: UST 曲線以 空頭壓平 收盤：2Y 4.08%、10Y 4.49%、30Y 4.99%，2s10s 維持 41bp，5s30s 收斂至 78bp。
ADP 就業 +122k、ISM Services 54.5 高於預期，把 Fed swaps 年底升息機率推到 85%，油價與美元同時拉高政策溢價。
S&P 500 -0.7%、Nasdaq -0.9%、Dow -1.2%，WTI 收在 95.23 美元附近、黃金 -1.2%，風險資產開始驗證利率與能源壓力。
配置上維持 中性偏短 久期、偏好 5Y-10Y 相對 30Y，信用採高品質收益，TWD 海外債 ETF 等待 10Y 回到 4.45% 下方再加碼。

headline.primary: 油價與就業把 Fed 尾端風險推回前台
headline.secondary: UST 空頭壓平；10Y 4.49%、WTI 95.23、Fed swaps 年底升息機率約 85%。

sections.title: 一、美債收盤與曲線變化
sections.takeaway: UST 是前端與中段共同上移、長端相對黏著的 空頭壓平。
paragraphs: 美債收盤把盤面定義成 空頭壓平，而不是避險式 多頭壓平：2Y 收 4.08%、日升 3bp，5Y 收 4.21%、日升 4bp，10Y 收 4.49%、日升 3bp，30Y 收 4.99%、日升 2bp。2s10s 維持 41bp、5s30s 收斂 2bp 至 78bp，代表市場把油價、服務業與就業韌性先灌進政策路徑，長端雖仍貼近 5%，但相對沒有再明顯失控。
官方 CMT 的周變化也確認這不是單日雜訊：2Y 一周升 9bp、5Y 升 6bp、10Y 升 4bp，30Y 只升 1bp；月變化則是 2Y 與 5Y 各升 13bp，10Y 只升 4bp，30Y 反而低 3bp。這種結構代表市場正在把降息尾端拿掉，前端與中段 久期 的 carry 保護下降，曲線交易不宜只押長端單向走低。
就業數據是前端重新定價的第一個催化。ADP 顯示 5 月民間就業 +122k，薪資年增 4.4%，高於市場期待且是 2025 年 1 月以來最強的企業增員訊號；周五非農共識仍只有 +85k、失業率 4.3%、平均時薪 +0.3% m/m。若正式就業報告沒有顯著轉弱，2Y 與 5Y 對 Fed 升息選擇權的折價會繼續減少，10Y 下方 4.45% 的支撐也會變硬。
服務業數據把成長韌性與通膨壓力放在同一張曲線上。ISM Services 5 月為 54.5，高於約 53.7 的市場共識與 53.6 前值；新訂單升至 57.3，商業活動 57.7，但就業分項仍在 47.9。這個組合對利率的含義不是單純成長利多或衰退利多，而是需求尚未足以讓 Fed 放鬆、就業分項又提醒長端不能無限承受更高實質利率，因此 5Y 對 30Y 的相對壓力更清楚。
油價是第二個催化，WTI 收在 95.23 美元附近、日升約 1.8%，Brent 盤中仍在 98 美元上方。能源衝擊會先推升 CPI 預期與企業成本，再透過 Fed swaps 傳導到短端；同日黃金下跌 1.2% 至 4,434 美元，顯示盤面不是單純地緣避險，而是美元與實質利率重新抬頭。對 UST 而言，油價高位使 2Y-5Y 的風險補償比 20Y-30Y 更快調整。
跨資產收盤也驗證利率壓力開始影響風險承接。S&P 500 收 7,553.68、跌 0.7%，Nasdaq 收 26,853.98、跌 0.9%，Dow 收 50,687.07、跌 1.2%，九日漲勢中斷；DXY 升至 99.54、日升 0.4%。美股下跌沒有換來明顯 UST 買盤，代表風險資產回檔仍被油價與政策溢價抵消，久期 的防守功能短線下降。
供給面沒有成為主線，但長端接近 5% 仍是配置必須尊重的價格訊號。20Y 收 5.00%、30Y 收 4.99%，10s30s 收斂至 50bp，說明長端 期限溢價 沒有被風險資產下跌完全沖掉；當 30Y 在 5% 附近仍只能小幅跟跌或小幅跟升，保險、退休金與海外買盤的承接力會決定曲線能否從 空頭壓平 轉向更可交易的中段 多頭壓平。

sections.title: 二、美債走勢解讀
sections.takeaway: 定價邏輯從降息時程後移，轉成油價與就業共同維持 Fed 升息選擇權。
paragraphs: 這輪上行的核心不是成長恐慌，而是政策反應函數被迫變寬。Fed swaps 把年底 25bp 升息機率推到約 85%，一周前約 60%；這種變化會直接壓縮 2Y 與 5Y 的降息保費，使曲線前端不再把風險資產小跌視為買債理由。只要市場相信 Fed 需要保留升息語言，2s10s 即使沒有再倒掛擴大，也很難快速 steepen。
Williams 表示政策位置目前合適、沒有明顯升降息方向，Logan 則提醒若通膨與需求不降溫，今年仍可能需要升息。這兩種訊號合在一起，等於把 Fed 從單一路徑改成條件式反應：油價、關稅與 AI 投資若使金融條件維持寬鬆，前端要付更高保險；若就業資料轉弱，長端才有足夠理由把 10Y 拉回 4.45% 下方。
Beige Book 的訊息支持這個條件式定價。12 個地區中有 10 個顯示活動小幅至溫和擴張，能源、運輸、包材、食品與肥料成本仍被企業提到，製造業招聘也受到國防與資料中心需求支撐。這讓服務業 54.5 的成長訊號更難被解讀成短暫反彈，因為成本傳導與投資需求仍在，使 Fed 不容易以一次弱就業分項就轉鴿。
油價與地緣衝突對曲線的傳導主要在通膨風險，而不是立即的流動性壓力。美伊互有軍事行動、海灣主權風險升溫，WTI 即使自盤中高位回落仍在 95 美元上方；若企業把能源成本傳給服務價格，Fed 對二階效應的容忍度會下降。這使 5Y 成為最不舒服的 bucket，因為它同時反映未來幾次 FOMC 與中期通膨風險。
長端的黏著來自財政與全球 久期 的共同約束。30Y 收在 4.99%、20Y 收在 5.00%，並未因美股回落而明顯下行；英國通膨、歐洲貿易摩擦、日本匯率壓力與 EM 升息潮都限制了全球債券的同步牛市。對美債而言，這代表 10Y 接近 4.50% 時不宜只看風險資產，還要看海外長端是否能提供 長線資金 承接。
曲線交易應把這次視為前端政策溢價抬升後的短期壓縮，而非完整的長端重估。2s10s 月內收斂 9bp、5s30s 月內收斂 16bp，說明曲線已反映不少政策重定價；若周五非農只是符合 +85k 共識、薪資 +0.3% 且失業率 4.3%，曲線可能先在 10Y 4.45%-4.55% 區間消化，而不是立刻打開新一輪大幅 selloff。
相反地，若非農與初領失業金確認勞動市場沒有降溫，Fed swaps 85% 的升息機率就可能從尾端風險變成主場景，2Y 會比 30Y 更快重新定價。這會讓 carry book 承受負凸性：票息仍在，但價格波動集中在政策敏感段，單純拉長 久期 等待避險買盤的勝率下降。
另一個需要拆開看的因素是美元與黃金的背離。DXY 升至 99.54，黃金反而跌 1.2% 至 4,434 美元，表示市場沒有把地緣風險完全轉成傳統避險需求，而是把它放進美元、實質利率與前端政策溢價。這對曲線的含義是，若美元維持強勢，海外買盤即使看到 10Y 4.49% 的絕對收益，也可能因匯率避險成本而放慢進場速度。

sections.title: 三、其他市場動向
sections.takeaway: 跨市驗證集中在央行、信用一級市場、私募信貸 與 EM 資金條件的分化。
paragraphs: 英國與歐洲的訊號偏向限制全球 久期 多頭。OECD 預估 BOE 今年按兵不動、2027 年才降至 3.5%，同時把英國 CPI 看在 2026 年 3.7%、2027 年 2.4%，失業率 2026 年 5.5%、2027 年 5.3%，債務占 GDP 可能自 2023 年 98.8% 升至 2027 年 105.4%。這組數字意味 gilts 難以提供明確的全球領漲，UST 長端也少了一個外部 多頭壓平 錨。
歐中與美國關稅議題則透過商品、供應鏈與投資成本影響利率。歐盟準備對家庭與企業提示更強硬的中國貿易措施，美國也討論對多個貿易夥伴至少 10% 的進口關稅；這些不是單日 CPI 數字，卻會提高企業投入成本與庫存策略的不確定性。對債市而言，供應鏈再定價越像成本推動，前端越難因成長疑慮而快速轉鴿。
日本是亞洲利率與 FX 風險的核心。USD/JPY 接近 160，財務省官員保留干預語言，政府追加預算約 3.1 兆日圓，其中 2.5 兆日圓預留補貼與物價措施，且年度債券發行日曆暫不變。BOJ 溝通若比市場期待保守，日圓疲弱會提高進口通膨壓力；若轉向更明確升息，JGB 長端與海外避險成本又會壓縮日系資金配置美元債的空間。
澳洲與印度顯示亞洲本地資金條件並不一致。澳洲 7Y A$1bn 標售 bid-to-cover 超過 4 倍，反映 RBA 暫停訊號與 Q1 GDP 只增 0.3% 支撐中段債需求；但印度銀行貸款年增 16.2%，地方債銷售年減 11% 至 10.9 兆盧比，主權殖利率升至 7.04%。這代表亞洲 久期 不是整體買盤，資金會在成長放緩市場與高油價壓力市場之間重新分配。

信用一級市場仍有窗口，但品質區分非常明顯。Alphabet 透過 California Community Choice Financing Authority 進行 10 億美元預付能源免稅債，Bahrain 10Y 美元債在逾 32 億美元訂單下定價 7.125%、較初始指引收窄 37.5bp；同時海灣主權收入受油運與安全風險牽動。這說明高品質、稅務效率或主權收益仍能吸引資金，但信用買盤要求更清楚的收益補償。
AI 與資料中心相關融資持續支撐投資需求，卻也加劇信用分化。美國企業股權與債務供給被市場吸收，資料中心投資在澳洲 GDP 中也有明顯貢獻；但如果 AI 資本支出讓金融條件保持寬鬆，Fed 的通膨與需求風險會更難解除。對 credit 與 rates 的交集來說，AI 相關高品質發行可承接，不代表 HY 或軟體再融資可以用同樣利差通過。
私募信貸 與軟體貸款是風險端最需要區分的區域。部分軟體借款人面臨到期牆、AI 替代壓力與直接貸款人更挑剔的承作條件，老經濟中型企業反而被部分 distressed credit 投資人視為更可分析的收益來源。這個分化對債券 ETF 的含義是，投資級現金流與抵押品質仍可收收益，但 HY 軟體與槓桿貸款 曝險 不宜只因指數利差尚穩就放大部位。

EM 主權風險呈現油價、IMF 條款與本幣政策的三重差異。Zambia 債券約 81c，高於 78c 回購價，且超過 25% 的持有人群組足以影響回購條款；若 IMF 指標達成，票息可能自 0.5% 階梯升至 7.5%。Ghana CPI 5 月為 3.7%、低於 4.3% 預估，且外匯存底自 107 億美元升至 140 億美元，但食品與燃料仍會影響後續通膨。EM 硬通貨配置要看條款與商品收入，不宜只買高名目收益。
波蘭、南非、印尼、斯里蘭卡與其他 EM 央行的升息或偏緊訊號，說明地緣與油價已讓新興市場優先保護通膨可信度。波蘭央行在政策利率 3.75% 附近暫停，市場卻已反映不少收緊風險；這類本地市場的政策溢價會透過美元、油價與避險資金影響 UST 需求。若 EM 為守匯率而提高實質利率，美元債的相對吸引力會提高，但本幣債的匯率風險也會同步放大。
FX 風險不只在日圓。Morgan Stanley 提醒 6 月 FOMC 可能衝擊低波動與共識 carry，EUR、JPY、AUD、NZD 都在風險清單；DXY 日升 0.4% 已先把美元 曝險 拉回盤面。對亞洲投資人而言，美元走強短線保護 USD 資產報酬，也對 TWD 計價海外債 ETF 提供 NAV 換算助益，但實際報酬仍會同時受到美債價格與匯率避險成本牽動。

sections.title: 四、配置含義
sections.takeaway: 配置重點是降低追價、保留 5Y-10Y 相對價值，信用以高品質收益為主。
paragraphs: 久期 建議維持 中性偏短，而不是在 10Y 4.49% 立即大幅加碼。理由是 ADP +122k、ISM Services 54.5 與 WTI 95 美元共同支撐 Fed 升息選擇權，且 2Y 與 5Y 一周已升 9bp、6bp，政策敏感段尚未確認釋放壓力。若 10Y 重新跌破 4.45% 且 DXY 未續升，才把中段 久期 從中性拉回偏多。
曲線 上偏好 5Y-10Y 的相對價值，避免單純買 30Y。30Y 接近 4.99%、20Y 5.00%，看似收益吸引，但 5s30s 已收斂至 78bp、月內收斂 16bp，長端 期限溢價 未被完全壓下。若 30Y 站上 5.05% 且 10Y 未同步破 4.55%，才考慮分批增加長端；否則以 5Y-10Y 做主要 久期 表達，降低 fiscal 與供給風險。
信用配置維持 IG 與高品質金融、基建、能源效率相關收益，HY 曝險 暫不追。Alphabet 免稅能源融資與 Bahrain 7.125% 主權債顯示一級市場仍能承接清楚故事，但軟體 私募信貸、Zambia 條款與 EM 油價壓力提醒市場正在重估弱資產負債表。若 HY ETF 利差未給足油價與再融資風險，應把收益留在 IG、短中天期與有現金流抵押的結構。
EM 與亞洲利率配置要把油價與美元放在同一個 觸發門檻。印度銀行貸款 +16.2% 與主權殖利率 7.04% 說明本地資金成本正在上行；Ghana CPI 3.7% 低於預期則提供特定主權利多。配置上可以保留 EM 硬通貨小部位，但避免在美元升破 100、WTI 站回 98 美元上方時加碼本幣債，因為匯率防線會先吃掉 carry。
TWD 計價海外債 ETF 的操作應先拆 FX 換算、利率與利差。美債價格端受 10Y 4.45%-4.55% 區間牽動，美元走強通常提供 TWD NAV 換算助益，但若美債殖利率仍在高位盤整，利率損失可能抵銷該助益。加碼條件應設在 10Y 低於 4.45%、WTI 低於 92 美元、且利差穩定，三者至少兩項成立。
若需要維持收益曝險，較好的方式是用 barbell 降低單點政策風險：短端保留現金與短票的再投資彈性，中段用 5Y-10Y UST 或高評級美元債承接收益，長端只用小比例做 convexity。這樣可以在 Fed swaps 85% 升息機率回落時參與 多頭壓平，也能在非農強於 +85k、薪資高於 +0.3% 時避免前端與中段部位過度受損。
風險預算上，今天不宜把美股回落解讀成買債保證。S&P 500 -0.7%、Nasdaq -0.9% 仍不足以抵消油價與就業數據；若 Broadcom 或 AI 鏈進一步拖累科技股，但 WTI 與 DXY 同步維持高位，債券反而可能承受風險平價去槓桿。配置上應把 equity drawdown、oil shock、Fed 重新定價 分開管理，不用單一股債負相關假設。
對帳戶層級的風險控管，建議把觸發條件寫成可執行的三段式。第一段是 10Y 4.45% 下方才把 久期 從中性調成偏多；第二段是 WTI 92 美元下方才放寬對前端政策溢價的警戒；第三段是 DXY 若突破 100，視為 TWD NAV 的 FX 換算助益增強；但必須確認利率與利差未抵銷。若三段只成立一段，配置維持防守；若成立兩段以上，才把現金轉入中段債與 IG ETF。

sections.title: 五、後續觀察重點
sections.takeaway: 交易觸發集中在非農、油價、Fed 文字、10Y/30Y 關鍵位與信用發行品質。
paragraphs: 第一個 觸發門檻 是周五非農與周四初領失業金。若非農高於 +125k、失業率低於 4.3% 或平均時薪高於 +0.3% m/m，Fed swaps 的升息尾端風險會更像主場景，2Y 可能重新測試 4.15% 附近，5Y 也會壓回 4.25% 上方；若非農低於 +50k 且初領明顯上升，10Y 才有條件跌破 4.45%。
第二個 觸發門檻 是油價。WTI 若重新站上 98 美元，市場會把能源成本轉嫁與通膨二階效應放回 6 月 FOMC，2s10s 可能再度收斂；若 WTI 跌回 92 美元以下且 Brent 回到 95 美元下方，Fed 官員關於油價的防守語言會減弱，5Y-10Y 的 久期 才有較乾淨的反彈空間。
第三個 觸發門檻 是 10Y 4.45% / 4.55% 與 30Y 5.05%。10Y 站穩 4.55% 代表服務業與就業資料正在壓倒風險資產回檔，應降低中段追價；10Y 跌破 4.45% 且 30Y 沒有守在 5% 上方，才表示 長線資金 買盤重新控制曲線。30Y 若突破 5.05%，長端供給與 fiscal premium 會迫使 曲線 部位降槓桿。
第四個 觸發門檻 是 6/16-17 FOMC 聲明文字與官員發言。若聲明移除偏向降息的暗示、點陣圖或記者會保留升息條件，前端會重新付保費；若 Williams 類似的中性語言占上風且 Logan 的升息風險被淡化，市場可把 85% 升息機率視為過度定價。此時 2Y 低於 4.00% 才能確認降息保費回來。
第五個 觸發門檻 是信用一級市場品質。若 IG、金融與公用事業新券仍能縮窄定價，而 HY 軟體、私募信貸 相關借款人需要更高讓利，配置應維持高品質 barbell；若 Bahrain 類主權與 AI 相關融資同時需要明顯 concession，代表高利率開始穿透一級市場，信用 曝險 要比 久期 更早降風險。
第六個 觸發門檻 是美元與亞洲政策。DXY 若突破 100、USD/JPY 穩定高於 160，TWD 海外債 ETF 會有 FX 換算助益，但 BOJ 與財務省的反應仍會影響 JGB、美元避險成本與利率報酬；若日圓回到 158 下方且 DXY 回落，亞洲投資人需重新評估未避險美元債的 FX 助益是否減弱。這個條件成立前，海外債 ETF 加碼以分批和中天期為主。
第七個 觸發門檻 是市場能否把風險資產下跌重新轉成債券避險需求。若 S&P 500 再跌但 10Y 仍守在 4.50% 附近，代表油價與 Fed 定價仍壓過避險買盤，久期 不應追高；若股市續弱、WTI 回落且 DXY 失去上行動能，才是較乾淨的 傳統避險 rates rally。這個條件會決定 TWD 海外債 ETF 的加碼節奏。
第八個 觸發門檻 是供給與標售尾端。若後續 10Y 或 30Y 標售出現明顯 tail，且 bid-to-cover 低於近期均值，市場會把 20Y 5.00%、30Y 4.99% 解讀為仍不足以吸引長線資金，曲線 要防 長端 reset；若標售需求穩、油價回落，長端才可能重新成為 portfolio hedge。

en.title: Daily FI Market Tape | 2026-06-03 close
summary: The UST curve closed with bear flattening: 2Y 4.08%, 10Y 4.49%, 30Y 4.99%; 2s10s held at 41bp and 5s30s compressed to 78bp.
ADP employment at +122k and ISM Services at 54.5 above expectations pushed Fed swaps year-end hike probability to 85%, while oil and the dollar both lifted the policy premium.
S&P 500 -0.7%, Nasdaq -0.9%, Dow -1.2%; WTI closed near $95.23 and gold fell 1.2%, with risk assets starting to validate rates and energy pressure.

Allocation remains short-to-neutral Duration, with a preference for 5Y-10Y versus 30Y; credit stays in high-quality yield, while TWD overseas bond ETFs should wait for 10Y to move back below 4.45% before adding exposure.

headline.primary: Oil and Employment Put Fed Tail Risk Back in Focus
headline.secondary: UST bear flattening; 10Y 4.49%, WTI 95.23, Fed swaps year-end hike probability around 85%.

regime_strip.label: Curve
regime_strip.value: 5s30s tightened to 78bp; front end and belly pressured

regime_strip.label: USD
regime_strip.value: DXY firm but TWD firmer; FX signal split

regime_strip.label: ETF Lens
regime_strip.value: Separate FX, rates, and credit return sources first

positioning.label: Duration
positioning.best_expression: Scale into 5Y-10Y; do not chase the long end near 5%.
positioning.risk_trigger: Reduce risk if 10Y breaks above 4.55% or nonfarm payrolls exceed +125k.

positioning.label: Curve
positioning.view: Favor the belly versus the long end
positioning.best_expression: 5s30s has compressed to 78bp; avoid outright 30Y longs.
positioning.risk_trigger: Reduce leverage in long-end allocations if 30Y breaks above 5.05%.

positioning.label: Credit / ETF
positioning.view: IG first, disciplined on HY beta
positioning.best_expression: Prioritize high-quality yield and short-to-intermediate USD bonds.
positioning.risk_trigger: Trim if HY new-issue concessions widen or software refinancing pressure rises.

positioning.label: FX / NAV
positioning.best_expression: For TWD overseas bond ETFs, focus on scaling and hedging-cost control.
positioning.risk_trigger: A DXY break above 100 supports TWD NAV translation; add only if rates and spreads do not offset the FX benefit.

positioning.label: Add conditions
positioning.best_expression: Add only after at least two of the following hold: 10Y <4.45%, WTI <92, and DXY does not make a new high.
positioning.risk_trigger: Stay defensive if WTI >98 and Fed officials retain hike language.

driver_decomposition.signal: WTI 95.23, up around 1.8% on the day; Brent still traded above 98 intraday.
driver_decomposition.fi_impact: Inflation risk transmits first into 2Y-5Y, limiting a 10Y break below 4.45%.

driver_decomposition.signal: ADP +122k; ISM Services 54.5, above consensus near 53.7.
driver_decomposition.fi_impact: Fed swaps year-end hike probability is around 85%, pressuring policy-sensitive tenors.
The equity pullback did not generate haven buying in bonds, reducing Duration’s defensive role.

driver_decomposition.signal: BOE expected to stay on hold this year, JPY near 160, and several EM central banks skew hawkish.
driver_decomposition.fi_impact: Global rates cannot turn bullish in sync easily, leaving 30Y near 5% still requiring term premium.

driver_decomposition.signal: Bahrain 10Y at 7.125%, with orders above $3.2bn; Alphabet’s $1bn tax-exempt energy financing.
driver_decomposition.fi_impact: Yield demand remains, but markets are separating quality from refinancing pressure in pricing.

key_levels.label: 10Y UST
key_levels.detail: A downside break is needed to confirm belly demand; an upside break means reduce Duration.

key_levels.label: 30Y UST
key_levels.detail: A break higher would indicate long-end term premium is back in control.

key_levels.label: WTI
key_levels.detail: Below 92 would weaken the inflation premium; above 98 would add front-end pressure.

key_levels.label: DXY
key_levels.detail: A break higher is FX-translation positive for TWD overseas bond ETFs; confirm rates and spreads do not offset it.

event_risks.label: Nonfarm payrolls
event_risks.detail: Above +125k would reinforce the rate-hike tail risk.

event_risks.label: Initial jobless claims
event_risks.detail: Only a meaningful rise would give 10Y the fundamental basis to break below 4.45%.

event_risks.label: FOMC
event_risks.detail: Whether the statement removes rate-cut hints is central to front-end pricing.

asia_update.label: S&P 500 futures
asia_update.detail: Pre-Taipei risk appetite is carrying over pressure from the U.S. equity close.

asia_update.label: Oil Asia
asia_update.detail: Near $95 is still enough to sustain an inflation risk premium.

asia_update.label: JPY Asia
asia_update.detail: BOJ and Ministry of Finance signals are driving Asian rates.

rows.label: S&P 500
rows.note: A nine-day advance was interrupted as rates and oil simultaneously compressed risk appetite.

rows.label: Nasdaq
rows.note: Momentum in AI-related holdings cooled, with software stocks weighing on growth-stock valuations.

rows.label: Dow
rows.note: Cyclicals and value stocks fell together, showing the pressure was not limited to high-valuation technology.

rows.label: DXY
rows.note: The dollar was supported by Fed repricing, pressuring non-U.S. Duration and FX NAV.

rows.label: WTI
rows.note: Oil remains elevated, keeping the inflation risk premium embedded in front-end pricing.

rows.label: Gold
rows.note: A stronger dollar weighed on haven assets; the inflation trade did not fully rotate into haven buying.

rows.label: Credit proxy
rows.note: IG valuations need a wider spread cushion: Fed repricing and weaker risk tone argue for defense in intermediate and long credit duration.

rows.label: USD/TWD
rows.note: TWD strength trims the FX translation cushion for TWD NAV; a UST rally can offset the FX drag.

rows.label: Labor
rows.expected: Above market expectations; nonfarm payrolls consensus +85k, unemployment rate 4.3%
rows.note: Employment resilience has turned the rate-cut narrative into a hike option, with the front end most sensitive.

rows.label: ISM Services
rows.note: Services growth beat expectations, but the employment component stayed below 50, leaving the curve with both front-end pressure and growth resilience.

rows.label: Fed pricing
rows.value: Fed swaps year-end hike probability around 85%
rows.note: The policy reaction function has shifted from waiting for cuts to retaining hikes, pressuring 2Y and 5Y.

rows.label: Fed speakers
rows.value: Williams said policy is in the right place; Logan kept the possibility of a hike this year open
rows.note: Officials did not offer a one-way commitment, so the market must pay a premium for second-round effects from oil and tariffs.

rows.label: Beige Book
rows.value: 10/12 districts reported slight to modest expansion in activity
rows.note: Energy, transportation, packaging, and food costs still support the price narrative, limiting bull flattening.

rows.label: BOE / OECD
rows.value: BOE expected to stay on hold this year; cut to 3.5% in 2027
rows.note: The UK inflation and debt path make it difficult for gilts to lead a clear turn bullish in global Duration.

rows.label: Japan
rows.expected: Market still assessing BOJ June hike signals
rows.note: The combination of FX pressure and subsidies raises the difficulty of policy communication, with JGB spillovers into the UST long end.

rows.label: Funding / credit
rows.value: Alphabet $1bn prepaid energy tax-exempt bond; Bahrain 10Y 7.125%
rows.note: Demand remains for high-quality and sovereign yield, but funding costs are directly separating issuance quality.

rows.label: EM / Asia
rows.value: India bank loans +16.2%; state bond sales -11% to INR10.9tn
rows.note: Local funding is rotating from bonds into bank credit, making Asian rates beta more sensitive to oil and the dollar.

rows.label: Sovereign stress
rows.note: IMF terms, coupon step-ups, and commodity revenues still dominate EM hard-currency performance.

sections.title: 1. UST Close and Curve Moves
sections.takeaway: USTs saw bear flattening driven by a joint upward move in the front end and belly, with the long end relatively sticky.
paragraphs: The UST close defined the session as bear flattening rather than haven-style bull flattening: 2Y closed at 4.08%, up 3bp on the day; 5Y closed at 4.21%, up 4bp; 10Y closed at 4.49%, up 3bp; and 30Y closed at 4.99%, up 2bp. 2s10s held at 41bp and 5s30s compressed 2bp to 78bp, indicating that the market first priced oil, services, and employment resilience into the policy path. The long end remains close to 5%, but it did not lose control further in relative terms.

The weekly change in the official CMT series also confirms this was not one-day noise: 2Y rose 9bp over the week, 5Y rose 6bp, 10Y rose 4bp, and 30Y rose only 1bp. On a monthly basis, 2Y and 5Y are each up 13bp, 10Y is up only 4bp, and 30Y is lower by 3bp. This structure shows the market is removing the rate-cut tail. Carry protection in front-end and belly Duration has declined, and curve trades should not simply bet on a one-way decline in the long end.

Employment data was the first catalyst for front-end repricing. ADP showed May private payrolls at +122k and wage growth at 4.4% YoY, above market expectations and the strongest signal of corporate hiring since January 2025. Friday’s nonfarm payrolls consensus remains only +85k, with the unemployment rate at 4.3% and average hourly earnings at +0.3% m/m. If the official jobs report does not weaken materially, the discount applied to the Fed hike option in 2Y and 5Y will continue to shrink, and support below 4.45% in 10Y will harden.

Services data put growth resilience and inflation pressure on the same curve. ISM Services for May came in at 54.5, above market consensus near 53.7 and the prior 53.6. New orders rose to 57.3 and business activity to 57.7, while the employment component remained at 47.9. The rates implication of this mix is not simply pro-growth or pro-recession. Demand is still not weak enough for the Fed to ease, while the employment component reminds investors that the long end cannot absorb higher real rates indefinitely. That makes the relative pressure in 5Y versus 30Y clearer.

Oil was the second catalyst, with WTI closing near $95.23, up around 1.8% on the day, while Brent was still above $98 intraday. An energy shock first lifts CPI expectations and corporate costs, then transmits through Fed swaps into the short end. Gold fell 1.2% the same day to $4,434, showing the tape was not simply a geopolitical haven move, but a return of the dollar and real rates. For USTs, elevated oil prices mean risk compensation in 2Y-5Y adjusts faster than in 20Y-30Y.

The cross-asset close also confirmed that rates pressure is starting to affect risk absorption. The S&P 500 closed at 7,553.68, down 0.7%; the Nasdaq closed at 26,853.98, down 0.9%; and the Dow closed at 50,687.07, down 1.2%, breaking a nine-day advance. DXY rose to 99.54, up 0.4% on the day. The equity decline did not generate material UST buying, meaning the risk-asset pullback was still offset by oil and the policy premium, reducing Duration’s defensive function in the near term.

Supply was not the main theme, but the long end near 5% remains a price signal that allocations must respect. 20Y closed at 5.00% and 30Y at 4.99%, while 10s30s compressed to 50bp, showing that long-end term premium was not fully washed out by the decline in risk assets. When 30Y near 5% can still only follow slightly lower or slightly higher, the absorption capacity of insurers, pension funds, and overseas buyers will decide whether the curve can move from bear flattening into a more tradable belly-led bull flattening.

sections.title: 2. Interpreting the UST Move
sections.takeaway: The pricing logic has shifted from a delayed easing timetable to oil and employment jointly preserving the Fed hike option.
paragraphs: The core of this upward move is not growth panic, but a forced widening of the policy reaction function. Fed swaps pushed the probability of a 25bp hike by year-end to around 85%, versus roughly 60% one week ago. This shift directly compresses the rate-cut premium in 2Y and 5Y, so the front end no longer treats a modest risk-asset decline as a reason to buy bonds. As long as the market believes the Fed needs to retain hike language, 2s10s will struggle to steepen quickly even if inversion does not extend further.

Williams said the current policy stance is appropriate and offered no clear direction on hikes or cuts, while Logan warned that a hike could still be needed this year if inflation and demand do not cool. Taken together, these signals move the Fed from a single path to a conditional reaction function: if oil, tariffs, and AI investment keep financial conditions loose, the front end must pay more insurance; if labor data weakens, only then does the long end have enough basis to pull 10Y back below 4.45%.

The Beige Book supports this conditional pricing. Ten of 12 districts reported slight to modest activity expansion, with companies still citing energy, transportation, packaging, food, and fertilizer costs. Manufacturing hiring was also supported by defense and data-center demand. This makes the 54.5 services-growth signal harder to interpret as a temporary rebound, because cost pass-through and investment demand remain in place, making it difficult for the Fed to turn dovish on one weak employment sub-index.

The transmission from oil and geopolitical conflict into the curve is mainly through inflation risk, not immediate liquidity stress. The U.S. and Iran took mutual military actions, Gulf sovereign risk rose, and WTI remained above $95 even after falling from its intraday highs. If companies pass energy costs into services prices, the Fed’s tolerance for second-round effects will decline. This makes 5Y the most uncomfortable bucket, because it reflects both the next several FOMC meetings and medium-term inflation risk.

Long-end stickiness reflects the joint constraints of fiscal risk and global Duration. 30Y closed at 4.99% and 20Y at 5.00%, with no material rally despite the equity pullback. UK inflation, European trade friction, Japanese FX pressure, and the EM tightening cycle all limit a synchronized global bond bull market. For Treasuries, this means that when 10Y is near 4.50%, investors should look beyond risk assets and assess whether overseas long-end markets can provide real-money absorption.

Curve trades should view this episode as near-term compression after a rise in front-end policy premium, not a full long-end repricing. 2s10s has compressed 9bp over the month and 5s30s has compressed 16bp, indicating the curve has already reflected substantial policy repricing. If Friday’s nonfarm payrolls merely match consensus at +85k, wages at +0.3%, and unemployment at 4.3%, the curve may first consolidate in a 10Y 4.45%-4.55% range rather than immediately opening a new large selloff.

Conversely, if nonfarm payrolls and initial jobless claims confirm that the labor market has not cooled, the 85% hike probability in Fed swaps could move from tail risk to base case, with 2Y repricing faster than 30Y. That would expose carry books to negative convexity: coupons remain, but price volatility is concentrated in policy-sensitive tenors, reducing the win rate of simply extending Duration and waiting for haven demand.

Another factor that needs to be separated is the divergence between the dollar and gold. DXY rose to 99.54 while gold fell 1.2% to $4,434, showing that the market did not fully convert geopolitical risk into traditional haven demand. Instead, it embedded that risk into the dollar, real rates, and front-end policy premium. For the curve, the implication is that if the dollar stays firm, overseas buyers may slow their entry even when they see the absolute yield of 4.49% in 10Y, because FX hedging costs remain a constraint.

sections.title: 3. Other Market Developments
sections.takeaway: Cross-market confirmation is concentrated in central banks, credit primary markets, private credit, and diverging EM funding conditions.
paragraphs: Signals from the UK and Europe skew toward limiting global Duration bulls. The OECD expects the BOE to stay on hold this year and cut only to 3.5% in 2027, while forecasting UK CPI at 3.7% in 2026 and 2.4% in 2027, unemployment at 5.5% in 2026 and 5.3% in 2027, and debt-to-GDP rising from 98.8% in 2023 to 105.4% in 2027. This set of numbers means gilts are unlikely to provide a clear global lead rally, leaving the UST long end without an external bull-flattening anchor.

EU-China and U.S. tariff issues are affecting rates through goods, supply chains, and investment costs. The EU is preparing to advise households and companies on tougher trade measures toward China, while the U.S. is also discussing import tariffs of at least 10% on several trade partners. These are not one-day CPI prints, but they increase uncertainty around corporate input costs and inventory strategies. For bond markets, the more supply-chain repricing looks like cost-push inflation, the harder it is for the front end to turn dovish quickly on growth concerns.

Japan is the core of Asian rates and FX risk. USD/JPY is near 160, Ministry of Finance officials have retained intervention language, and the government has added a supplementary budget of about JPY3.1tn, including JPY2.5tn reserved for subsidies and price measures, while the annual bond issuance calendar is temporarily unchanged. If BOJ communication is more conservative than markets expect, yen weakness will lift import-inflation pressure. If the BOJ shifts toward a clearer hike, long-end JGBs and overseas hedging costs will compress the room for Japanese capital to allocate to USD bonds.

Australia and India show that local Asian funding conditions are not uniform. Australia’s A$1bn 7Y auction had a bid-to-cover ratio above 4x, reflecting demand for belly bonds supported by the RBA pause signal and Q1 GDP growth of only 0.3%. But India’s bank loans rose 16.2% YoY, state bond sales fell 11% YoY to INR10.9tn, and sovereign yields rose to 7.04%. This means Asian Duration is not a broad-based bid. Capital will be reallocated between markets with slowing growth and markets under pressure from high oil prices.

The credit primary window remains open, but quality differentiation is very clear. Alphabet executed $1bn of prepaid energy tax-exempt bonds through the California Community Choice Financing Authority, while Bahrain priced 10Y USD bonds at 7.125% on more than $3.2bn of orders, 37.5bp tighter than initial guidance. At the same time, Gulf sovereign revenues are tied to shipping and security risks. This shows that high quality, tax efficiency, or sovereign yield can still attract capital, but credit buyers are demanding clearer yield compensation.

AI and data-center-related financing continues to support investment demand, but it is also intensifying credit dispersion. U.S. corporate equity and debt supply has been absorbed by the market, and data-center investment also made a visible contribution to Australian GDP. But if AI capex keeps financial conditions loose, the Fed’s inflation and demand risks will be harder to remove. At the intersection of credit and rates, demand for high-quality AI-related issuance does not mean HY or software refinancing can clear at the same spreads.

Private credit and software loans are the areas of the risk complex that require the most differentiation. Some software borrowers face maturity walls, AI substitution pressure, and more selective underwriting standards from direct lenders, while parts of the old-economy middle market are being viewed by some distressed credit investors as more analyzable sources of yield. For bond ETFs, the implication is that investment-grade cash flows and collateral quality can still harvest yield, but HY software and leveraged-loan beta should not be scaled up simply because index spreads remain stable.

EM sovereign risk shows a three-way divergence across oil prices, IMF terms, and local policy. Zambia bonds are around 81c, above the 78c buyback price, and a holder group with more than 25% is enough to influence buyback terms. If IMF metrics are met, coupons could step up from 0.5% to 7.5%. Ghana CPI was 3.7% in May, below the 4.3% estimate, and FX reserves rose from $10.7bn to $14.0bn, but food and fuel will still affect future inflation. EM hard-currency allocation should focus on terms and commodity revenues, rather than simply buying high nominal yield.

Hike or hawkish signals from Poland, South Africa, Indonesia, Sri Lanka, and other EM central banks show that geopolitics and oil prices have pushed emerging markets to prioritize protection of inflation credibility. Poland’s central bank paused with the policy rate near 3.75%, but the market has already reflected considerable tightening risk. This type of local-market policy premium affects UST demand through the dollar, oil, and risk capital. If EM raises real rates to defend currencies, the relative appeal of USD bonds rises, but FX risk in local-currency bonds increases at the same time.

FX risk is not only about the yen. Morgan Stanley warned that the June FOMC could disrupt low-volatility and consensus carry trades, with EUR, JPY, AUD, and NZD all on the risk list. DXY’s 0.4% daily rise has already brought dollar beta back onto the tape. For Asian investors, a stronger dollar protects USD-asset returns in the short term and supports TWD-denominated overseas bond ETF NAV via translation, but actual returns will still be driven simultaneously by UST prices and FX hedging costs.

sections.title: 4. Allocation Implications
sections.takeaway: The allocation focus is to reduce chasing, preserve relative value in 5Y-10Y, and keep credit exposure centered on high-quality yield.
paragraphs: Duration should remain short-to-neutral, rather than adding heavily as soon as 10Y reaches 4.49%. The reason is that ADP +122k, ISM Services 54.5, and WTI at $95 jointly support the Fed hike option, while 2Y and 5Y have already risen 9bp and 6bp over the week, with policy-sensitive tenors not yet confirmed to have released pressure. Only if 10Y falls back below 4.45% and DXY does not continue rising should belly Duration move from neutral back toward overweight.

On the curve, favor relative value in 5Y-10Y and avoid simply buying 30Y. 30Y near 4.99% and 20Y at 5.00% look yield-attractive, but 5s30s has already compressed to 78bp and by 16bp over the month, while long-end term premium has not been fully suppressed. If 30Y rises above 5.05% and 10Y does not simultaneously break 4.55%, then consider gradually increasing long-end exposure. Otherwise, use 5Y-10Y as the main Duration expression to reduce fiscal and supply risk.

Credit allocation should remain in IG and high-quality financials, infrastructure, and energy-efficiency-related yield, while not chasing HY beta for now. Alphabet’s tax-exempt energy financing and Bahrain’s 7.125% sovereign bond show the primary market can still absorb clear stories, but software private credit, Zambia terms, and EM oil-price pressure show that the market is repricing weaker balance sheets. If HY ETF spreads do not adequately compensate for oil and refinancing risk, keep yield in IG, short-to-intermediate maturities, and structures backed by cash-flow collateral.

EM and Asian rates allocation should put oil and the dollar into the same trigger framework. India bank loans at +16.2% and sovereign yields at 7.04% show local funding costs are rising, while Ghana CPI at 3.7% below expectations provides specific sovereign support. Allocation can retain small EM hard-currency positions, but should avoid adding to local-currency bonds when the dollar breaks above 100 and WTI moves back above $98, because the FX defense line will consume carry first.

For TWD-denominated overseas bond ETFs, NAV defense should lead the operation. The UST price side is driven by the 10Y 4.45%-4.55% range, while the FX side is affected by DXY at 99.54 and hedging costs with the yen near 160. If UST yields are merely consolidating at elevated levels, the income seen by TWD investors may not be fully retained. Conditions for adding should be set at 10Y below 4.45%, WTI below $92, and DXY no longer making new highs, with at least two of the three satisfied.

If yield exposure must be maintained, the better approach is to use a barbell to reduce single-point policy risk: keep cash and short bills at the front end for reinvestment flexibility, use 5Y-10Y USTs or high-grade USD bonds in the belly to harvest yield, and use only a small allocation to the long end for convexity. This allows participation in bull flattening if the 85% hike probability in Fed swaps falls back, while avoiding excessive damage in front-end and belly positions if nonfarm payrolls beat +85k and wages exceed +0.3%.

In risk budgeting, today’s equity decline should not be read as a guaranteed bond-buying signal. S&P 500 -0.7% and Nasdaq -0.9% were still not enough to offset oil and employment data. If Broadcom or the AI chain further weighs on technology shares while WTI and DXY remain elevated together, bonds may instead face risk-parity deleveraging. Allocation should manage equity drawdown, oil shock, and Fed repricing separately, without relying on a single negative stock-bond correlation assumption.

At the account level, risk controls should be written as an executable three-stage framework. The first stage is to move Duration from neutral to overweight only below 4.45% in 10Y; the second is to relax caution on front-end policy premium only below $92 in WTI; and the third is to treat a DXY break above 100 as stronger FX support for TWD NAV while confirming rates and spreads do not offset it. If only one of the three stages is met, keep allocations defensive. If two or more are met, then rotate cash into belly bonds and IG ETFs.

sections.title: 5. Watchlist
sections.takeaway: Trading triggers are concentrated in nonfarm payrolls, oil, Fed language, key 10Y/30Y levels, and credit issuance quality.
paragraphs: The first trigger is Friday’s nonfarm payrolls and Thursday’s initial jobless claims. If payrolls exceed +125k, the unemployment rate is below 4.3%, or average hourly earnings exceed +0.3% m/m, the hike tail risk in Fed swaps will look more like the base case, with 2Y potentially retesting the 4.15% area and 5Y moving back above 4.25%. If payrolls are below +50k and initial claims rise meaningfully, only then would 10Y have the conditions to break below 4.45%.

The second trigger is oil. If WTI moves back above $98, the market will bring energy cost pass-through and second-round inflation effects back into the June FOMC, and 2s10s could compress again. If WTI falls back below $92 and Brent returns below $95, Fed officials’ defensive language on oil will weaken, giving 5Y-10Y Duration a cleaner rebound window.

The third trigger is 10Y 4.45% / 4.55% and 30Y 5.05%. If 10Y holds above 4.55%, services and employment data are overpowering the risk-asset pullback, and investors should reduce chasing in the belly. Only if 10Y breaks below 4.45% and 30Y does not stay above 5% would it indicate real-money demand has regained control of the curve. If 30Y breaks above 5.05%, long-end supply and fiscal premium will force curve positions to reduce leverage.

The fourth trigger is the 6/16-17 FOMC statement language and official commentary. If the statement removes rate-cut-leaning hints, or the dot plot or press conference retains hike conditions, the front end will reprice premium. If neutral language similar to Williams dominates and Logan’s hike risk is downplayed, the market can treat the 85% hike probability as overpricing. At that point, 2Y below 4.00% would be needed to confirm that rate-cut premium has returned.

The fifth trigger is credit primary-market quality. If IG, financials, and utilities new issues can still price tighter while HY software and private-credit-related borrowers require larger concessions, allocation should maintain a high-quality barbell. If Bahrain-like sovereigns and AI-related financing both require meaningful concession, that would mean high rates are starting to penetrate the primary market, and credit beta should be de-risked earlier than Duration.

The sixth trigger is the dollar and Asian policy. If DXY breaks above 100 and USD/JPY stabilizes above 160, TWD overseas bond ETFs get FX translation support, but the BOJ and Ministry of Finance response will still affect JGBs, USD hedging costs, and rates returns. If the yen returns below 158 and DXY retreats, Asian investors should reassess whether the FX benefit to unhedged USD bond income is fading. Before that condition holds, additions to overseas bond ETFs should be phased and concentrated in intermediate maturities.

The seventh trigger is whether the market can convert risk-asset weakness back into bond haven demand. If the S&P 500 falls further but 10Y still holds near 4.50%, oil and Fed pricing are still overpowering haven demand, and investors should not chase Duration. If equities weaken further, WTI retreats, and DXY loses upward momentum, that would be a cleaner risk-off rates rally. This condition will determine the pace of adding to TWD overseas bond ETFs.

The eighth trigger is supply and auction tails. If upcoming 10Y or 30Y auctions show a clear tail and bid-to-cover falls below recent averages, the market will read 20Y at 5.00% and 30Y at 4.99% as still insufficient to attract long-term capital, and the curve needs to guard against a long-end reset. If auction demand is stable and oil falls, the long end can become a portfolio hedge again.
